items.q: Which of these is a ball-and-socket joint?
items.why: Ball-and-socket joints allow movement in many directions; the hip and shoulder are the main examples.

items.correct: Bone to bone, stabilising a joint.
items.why: Ligaments join bones across a joint; tendons are what connect muscle to bone.

items.q: What type of joint is the knee?
items.correct: A hinge joint, allowing flexion and extension.
items.why: The knee bends in one plane like a hinge, so it is not a multi-directional ball-and-socket joint.

items.q: How many bones are in the adult human skeleton?
items.why: Correct answer: 206. “106” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: Hip. “Elbow” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Number of bones in the adult human body?
items.why: The real figure is 206 bones. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
